name: spotify
description: Control {user}'s Spotify (unofficial API): browse playlists, search and play specific songs, manage the queue, run transport (pause/resume/skip/shuffle/repeat/volume), and list or choose the playback device. Use whenever {user} says 'play <song>', 'put on <playlist>', 'pause/skip/next', 'turn it up', 'what's playing', 'my playlists', or 'play on my <device>'. KEY GOTCHA: playback control runs over Spotify Connect, so Spotify must already be OPEN/active on a device; browsing and search work regardless.
instructions:
# Spotify control (unofficial API via SpotAPI)

Drives {user}'s Spotify the way the web player does — no Spotify Developer app, no official OAuth, just their logged-in session (cookies). Lets Alistair browse, search, play, queue, run transport, and pick the playback device.

## The one rule that trips people up
**Playback control uses Spotify Connect, which only commands devices that are already online.** So play / pause / skip / volume / transfer need Spotify ALREADY OPEN and active somewhere (phone, desktop, or web player). If `spotify_devices` is empty or a control call returns a 409 'no active device', tell {user} to open Spotify on a device and try again — do NOT pretend it worked. Browsing (`spotify_playlists`, `spotify_playlist_tracks`) and `spotify_search` work with no active device.

## The play flow (turn 'play X' into action)
1. `spotify_search('<song> <artist>')` → pick the right result, take its `uri`.
2. To play it inside a playlist's context: get the playlist uri (`spotify_playlists`), then `spotify_play(track=<track uri>, playlist=<playlist uri>)`. This is the proper Spotify behaviour (it plays the track within that playlist).
3. To just play one song with no playlist: `spotify_play(track=<uri>)` — it queues the track and skips to it.
4. To play on a specific device: add `device=<id or name>` to `spotify_play`, or move first with `spotify_transfer`.

## Tools
- `spotify_playlists` — {user}'s library playlists (name, uri, owner, track count). Read-only.
- `spotify_playlist_tracks(playlist)` — the tracks in a playlist (name, artists, album, track uri). Read-only.
- `spotify_search(query)` — catalogue search → track uris. Read-only.
- `spotify_devices` — available Connect devices + which is active. Read-only.
- `spotify_transfer(device)` — move playback to a device (id, or a unique part of its name).
- `spotify_status` — what's playing now + active device + shuffle/repeat + up-next. Read-only.
- `spotify_play(track, playlist?, device?)` — play a specific track (see flow above).
- `spotify_queue(track)` — add a track to the queue without interrupting.
- `spotify_control(action, value?)` — pause | resume | next | previous | restart | shuffle_on | shuffle_off | repeat_on | repeat_off | volume (value=0-100) | seek (value=ms).

## Choosing a device
List with `spotify_devices`, then pass the device id OR a unique fragment of its name to `spotify_transfer` / `spotify_play(device=...)`. If a name matches more than one device, the tool asks you to be specific — surface the options to {user} and confirm rather than guessing.

## Etiquette
- These are REAL actions on {user}'s actual devices (they start/stop audio). They're reversible (pause, transfer back), so no hard confirm gate — but confirm an ambiguous device, and never fabricate 'now playing'; read `spotify_status`.
- volume is a percent 0-100; seek is milliseconds.

## If something fails
- **503 'Spotify is not configured'** → set SPOTIFY_COOKIES (open.spotify.com cookies, at least sp_dc) + SPOTIFY_USERNAME in Railway.
- **503 'needs the spotapi[websocket] package'** → the dependency didn't install; check the build.
- **409 'no active Spotify device'** → open Spotify on a device and start playback, then retry.
- **401 / session errors after a while** → the cookies expired; refresh SPOTIFY_COOKIES from a fresh browser login.

sections.heading: Active-device rule
sections.body: Playback control (play/pause/skip/volume/transfer/queue) runs over Spotify Connect, so Spotify must already be OPEN on a device. If `spotify_devices` is empty or a call returns 409, tell {user} to open Spotify and retry — don't fake success. Browsing and search work without an active device.

sections.heading: Play flow
sections.body: `spotify_search` → take the track uri → `spotify_play(track=<uri>, playlist=<uri optional>, device=<optional>)`. With a playlist it plays in that context; without one it queues+skips. `spotify_queue` adds without interrupting.

sections.heading: Devices
sections.body: `spotify_devices` lists devices + the active one. `spotify_transfer(device)` or `spotify_play(device=...)` accepts an id or a unique name fragment. Ambiguous name → the tool asks for specificity; confirm with {user}.

sections.heading: Transport
sections.body: `spotify_control(action, value?)`: pause/resume/next/previous/restart, shuffle_on/off, repeat_on/off, volume (value=0-100), seek (value=ms). `spotify_status` is 'what's playing'.

sections.heading: If something fails
sections.body: 503 not configured → set SPOTIFY_COOKIES + SPOTIFY_USERNAME. 503 package missing → spotapi[websocket] didn't install. 409 no active device → open Spotify and retry. 401/session → cookies expired; refresh them.
